subtitle: Ross vs. Grassi — Malaria
summary: Ronald Ross, a failed writer and frustrated army doctor, returns to London in 1894 and meets Patrick Manson, who convinces him that mosquitoes carry malaria. Ross sails to India to prove the theory, but his early experiments are plagued by ignorance, bungling, and stubborn mosquitoes that refuse to bite. Despite years of failed attempts feeding people mosquito soup and chasing false leads at Secunderabad, Ross persists with relentless determination, slowly learning to identify malaria parasites even as the real answer eludes him.

segments.anchor_sentence: He went back to London in 1894, plotting to throw up medicine and science.

visual.scene_description: Ross and Manson peer through a microscope in Manson's London office, watching crescent-shaped malaria parasites with whip-like flagella bursting from red blood cells.

segments.anchor_sentence: Of course that didn't really bother Patrick Manson at all.

visual.scene_description: Manson and Ross walk along busy Oxford Street as Manson passionately explains his theory that mosquitoes transmit malaria, gesturing toward an imagined mosquito.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, two Victorian gentlemen walking along a crowded London street with horse-drawn carriages and gas lamps, older doctor in black frock coat gesturing emphatically with both hands, younger man with thin mustache listening intently, a large detailed mosquito looming above them in the sky like a shared vision, brick storefronts and top-hatted pedestrians in background

segments.anchor_sentence: "It is my duty to solve the problem," Ross said.

visual.scene_description: Ross arrives at the desolate military station at Secunderabad, a sun-scorched plain with rocky outcrops and small lakes, carrying his microscope case and looking determined.

segments.anchor_sentence: He started out to follow Manson's orders.

visual.scene_description: Ross in his cramped hot quarters at Secunderabad, hunched over a mosquito net soaked with water, trying to coax mosquitoes to bite while peering at captured specimens in glass bottles.

segments.anchor_sentence: What an incredible pair of searchers they were!

visual.scene_description: Ross at his desk writing frantic letters to Manson, surrounded by bottles of dead mosquitoes and failed experiments, a glass of murky mosquito-water nearby.
